questions.text: When it comes to getting around — do you own a car where you are now (Mexico), or do you rely entirely on ride-shares, public transit, and walking? And what's your general philosophy on car ownership — is it something you see yourself doing eventually, or do you prefer the flexibility of not having one?

Beyond just 'sustainability as a value' — when you're actually buying something (clothes, tech, food, household stuff), how often does sustainability factor into the decision? Are there specific things you actively avoid (fast fashion, certain brands), things you seek out (eco-certifications, secondhand), or is it more of a 'I think about it sometimes' kind of thing?

When it comes to community and local involvement — volleyball with friends is your current thing, but do you ever see yourself getting more involved in your communities (wherever you're living)? Like, volunteering, local organizations, meetups, religious groups, or anything where you're showing up regularly as a regular part of something bigger?

When you want to learn something new — not for work specifically, but like a personal skill or topic you're curious about — how do you actually go about it? Do you dive into YouTube tutorials, read books, take online courses, tinker around, ask people, or something else? What's your go-to method for learning outside of structured education?

When you're just scrolling or consuming content for fun — beyond podcasts and music — what's your vibe? Do you spend time on Reddit, YouTube, TikTok, Twitter/X, news apps, or do you find yourself mostly just doing other things? How do you unwind with content when you're not actively seeking something specific?
